title: Rogersonanthus arboreus
taxonomy: Rogersonanthus arboreus is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It is native to South Africa. This plant is part of the genus Rogersonanthus, which is closely related to the genus Hesperantha. Rogersonanthus arboreus is a climbing or scrambling plant with a woody stem. It is often used as a groundcover or in hanging baskets due to its spreading habit.

intro: Rogersonanthus arboreus, also known as the Forest Sage, is a woody plant native to the tropical regions of southern Africa, belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and support immune function.
